channel.title: Giwan's Blog
channel.description: Personal technical blog by Giwan – built with Astro.

item.title: Solo Dev Force Multipliers: Mastering the Agent Skills Ecosystem
item.description: Stop using AI agents as simple chatbots. Learn how to use Agent Skills to codify complex workflows, automate design systems, and manage specialized subagents.

item.title: Best AI Coding Models 2026 in OpenCode: Comparing Free Options
item.description: Compare free AI models (Kimi, MiniMax, Trinity) available via OpenCode CLI with Gemini 3 Pro and Claude Opus 4.5. Find the best AI coding assistant in 2026.

item.title: Essential Google AI Tools for Developers (2026)
item.description: Explore Google's AI developer ecosystem in 2026: Gemini API, Stitch UI generator, Antigravity IDE, Jules coding agent, and NotebookLM for research.

item.title: Universal Commerce Protocol: The New Language of AI Shopping
item.description: A critical look at Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP). While it promises a unified standard for AI agents, it raises questions about privacy and centralization.

item.title: Free AI Courses 2026 (NVIDIA, Hugging Face, Google & Microsoft)
item.description: Master AI development in 2026 with free courses from NVIDIA, Hugging Face, Google & Microsoft. Learn Agents, RAG, and LLMs—no fluff, just code.

item.title: Hacking the AI Vending Machine: Security Lessons from Project Vend
item.description: Anthropic's Project Vend Phase Two was a controlled experiment designed to expose the edge cases and security vulnerabilities of autonomous AI agents.

item.title: The Windows 11 Crisis
item.description: Exploring the concerns raised about Windows 11 regarding hardware requirements, forced AI integration, and planned obsolescence.

item.title: How RemixJS Works: A Deep Dive
item.description: Explore the core architecture of RemixJS: The Compiler, Server Handler, Server Framework, and Browser Framework in this deep dive.

item.title: Building Basic Pomodoro with Antigravity
item.description: A quick look at building the basic-pomo project using the Antigravity IDE.

item.title: Global Giants and European Leaders Blocking OpenAI's Bot
item.description: A look at the major websites, including European and Dutch companies, that are blocking OpenAI's GPTBot and why they are doing it.

item.title: Note-Taking Tools for Web Developers in 2025
item.description: A developer-focused guide to the best note-taking tools for technical documentation, code snippets, project planning, and knowledge management. Find your perfect coding companion.

item.title: Sentence Segmentation with JavaScript's Intl.Segmenter
item.description: Explore how the Intl.Segmenter API can be used to split text into sentences.

item.title: How Claude Code Helped Ship an Image Generation Feature in Half the Time
item.description: I leveraged Claude Code to implement an AI image generation feature for Mytoori, comparing different AI models and sharing key learnings.

item.title: Prompt Engineering: Essential Do's and Don'ts for Beginners
item.description: Master the art of prompt engineering with these essential do's and don'ts to get the best results from AI language models.

item.title: Deploy Astro Static on Deno Deploy
item.description: With a little bit of workaround it's possible to deploy a static Astro site to deno deploy. Initially Astro's docs indicated only SSR was supported.

item.title: Applicants demo app
item.description: This is a simple demo app that get's a list of applicants from the server. Those applicants are listed in a table. The user can filter and sort them.

item.title: Finding tools on the web 🌍
item.description: Finding tools on the web is getting increasingly difficult with a regular search engine. This post talks about some of the places on the web that make this easy.

item.title: Recursive JS function
item.description: Loop over an array with a recursive function. In this case the array entry has a URL that should be validated before moving on to the next

item.title: The basics of publishing on NPM
item.description: The pagination component created in another post will be published on NPM. It allows others to use the component in their React projects.

item.title: Pagination component in React
item.description: Let's build a pagination component. It should adhere to some basic UI interaction requirements. The goal is deliver an extra light component.

item.title: Electron's main and renderer process communication
item.description: After setting up a basic ElectronJS, this article discusses the preload.js script. It's used to communicate between the main electron process and the unsafe renderer process. The latter is not allowed to access the fs API for security reasons.

item.title: Basic ElectronJS project using React
item.description: Setup a basic electron (desktop) app using snowpack for ReactJS. This article demonstrates how to get up and running.No webpack and no create-react-app.

item.title: VueJS introduction: Solar system
item.description: Not having worked very much with VueJS, this is a simple app that goes through the basics. It covers architecture and the way information is shared between components.

item.title: NextJS blogging with MDX
item.description: NextJS is great for blogging. MDX adds more power to that by allowing JSX components to be directly rendered in the blog. This post will discuss setup and show examples.

item.title: WhatsApp issued an ultimatum!
item.description: The ball is in our court: 'Accept the TOS or stop using WhatsApp' says Facebook. Most people might be OK with this. I'm not. Here are some good alternatives.

item.title: Animation basics. Radar animation screen.
item.description: A look at making some basic animations using vanilla JS. Creating a radar animation demonstrates the basics.

item.title: Store your resume as a JSON file
item.description: Store your resume in a JSON doc and then apply various styles to it

item.title: Using a function instead of a loop
item.description: Using for loops is basic however JS offers several ways of looping with functions.

item.title: Browser debugging with breakpoints (React)
item.description: Breakpoints are great but seem to be troublesome in some cases

item.title: Deploy a React SPA with serverless functions to Netlify
item.description: Deploy and test the project directly from the remote server.

item.title: Getting started with serverless functions on Netlify
item.description: Setup a simple demo login function to see serverless functions in action

item.title: Using UUIDs as passwords?
item.description: What if a user's passwords were a list of UUIDs instead? A list that can be generated in a less traditional means.

item.title: JavaScript's Uncanny Valley
item.description: The uncanny valley is time needed for browsers to process JS code and build your application

item.title: Server side rendering with React & Redux
item.description: When handling server side rendering, the Redux data should be populated as well

item.title: Svelte Introduction
item.description: A small demo project to work on Svelte. Svelte is a UI Framework, like ReactJS or ViewJS. However it takes a different approach by acting as a compiler.

item.title: Browser dev tools
item.description: The dev tools in the browser grow more advanced every day. This post keeps track of some of the most useful features. Where possible key differences between browsers is highlighted. Most features are in Chrome on MacOS.

item.title: CSS grid
item.description: CSS grid makes it a pleasure to create layouts. Here we'll create a resume using CSS grid. Only HTML and CSS will be used. No JavaScript required. Another goal is to keep it all in one file to make it easy to distribute and fast.

item.title: Jest coverage in Create React App
item.description: Configure Jest coverage in Create React App. Coverage indicates how much of the code is tested. A higher than 80% coverage is a decent indicator that the code is working as expected.

item.title: Free static site
item.description: How to create and host a static site, including SSL and domain name for 0 euros! Static sites can be hosted for free thanks to some amazing services available as of 2018.

item.title: React App Served by Express in Docker
item.description: Running in Docker requires special considerations in the front-end. It should dynamically retrieve the backend end-point. For multiple environments, the same docker image could be used while moving from TST to ACC for example.

item.title: React Server Pages
item.description: React can replace Java Server Pages (JSP), HandlebarJS, etc. as a templating language on the server.

item.title: Server Side Rendering - React & NodeJS
item.description: Web crawlers can't parse JS which means that Single Page Applications need to render on the server if they want to be indexed by the search engines.

item.title: Debugging with Visual Studio Code
item.description: How debugging works with visual studio code. It's especially useful when debugging server side projects like NodeJS. Set breakpoint in the editor while developing.
